steps.title: Identify the ranking effect in the data
steps.content: The conclusion predicts ranking makes people focus on the top choice. The British Museum ranks #1 and received 36% of reading time in the ranked group—nearly double its 18% in the unranked group. This disproportionate spike in attention to the top-ranked option is the key evidence.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content: Choice B states that the ranked group spent “disproportionately more time reading about the British Museum,” matching the 36%-vs-18% disparity shown in the table. The other choices describe the unranked group's roughly balanced behavior, an unrelated low value on one ranked museum, or a preference in the unranked condition. That matches choice B — B is correct.
